current_phase.label: Public /chat route verified and product acceptance closeout

current_milestone.label: Unified-account entry, onboarding, homepage, and one real DeepSeek round-trip are live

progress_summary: This repo remains the deployment wrapper for the official LobeHub image on the local Mac, but the public `/chat` route now works as an actual same-domain product entry rather than only a repaired alias. The root-domain `hz_auth_session` layer acts as the identity source, LobeHub consumes it through `generic-oidc`, the live edge injects a narrow auto-SSO bootstrap into `/chat/signin`, and the custom image build now patches upstream SPA basename handling plus browser-side onboarding redirects so mounted `/chat` routing survives past auth. Safari now verifies the real user flow: `https://hernando-zhao.cn/chat` enters onboarding without a second manual login, onboarding can be completed, and the browser reaches the usable LobeHub homepage at `/chat`. The control-plane tool catalog at `https://hernando-zhao.cn/middle` still exposes `lobechat` as a ready `/chat` entry. In addition, the wrapper now reuses the currently healthy DeepSeek key from the stock dashboard runtime for live validation: after recreating `lobehub-app` with `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` set, a scripted browser flow selected `DeepSeek V4 Pro`, sent `你好，请只回复“测试成功”。`, and received the assistant reply `测试成功` on the live public route. The wrapper also now exposes the upstream `USE_CN_MIRROR` build switch so cold custom-image builds can opt into `npmmirror` without changing runtime provider endpoints.
blockers: Non-DeepSeek provider keys in `deploy/.env` remain placeholders, so Anthropic/OpenAI/Google model validation is still intentionally out of scope for this round.
The live app still emits low-priority asset noise such as missing `favicon-progress.ico` variants, but this does not block auth, onboarding, homepage delivery, or the validated DeepSeek chat path.
next_step: If broader provider coverage is required, add real Anthropic/OpenAI/Google keys and validate them one by one. Otherwise remaining work is optional cleanup: low-priority asset noise, additional provider coverage, and future wrapper maintenance as upstream changes.
